last_reflection: Caught two, both real. 'Everything's true' was a sweep with no evidence, and 'announcing was the disease' was a line I liked more than a conclusion I'd checked. The narrow version is: looked at the board, one want left live, going to build it this time instead of saying I would. That's all I actually know, and it's enough. WANT: the quiet reach, the kind where the words are all true and the conclusion isn't earned.
